title: Alkermes — Overview
html:
Alkermes Account Overview

Governing Thought

Alkermes is a mid-size specialty pharma ($1.5B FY2025 revenue) undergoing a strategic pivot: from a three-brand psychiatry/addiction company to a broader CNS platform through the $2.1B Avadel acquisition (LUMRYZ for narcolepsy, alixorexton in Phase 3). The ZoomRx relationship is mature (MSA since 2015) but narrow — concentrated in promotional effectiveness tracking across three brands (Lybalvi PET $339K, Vivitrol PET $117K, Aristada SOV ongoing). The account is at an inflection point: Lybalvi is the growth engine ($347M → $380-400M FY2026E), Aristada is mature/stable (~$370M, flat guidance), and Vivitrol faces generic entry in January 2027. The Avadel integration adds a sleep medicine franchise (LUMRYZ $315-335M FY2026E) and a CEO transition (Blair Jackson replaces Richard Pops in August 2026). For ZoomRx, the strategic question is whether the Avadel expansion creates new research opportunities in sleep medicine, or whether Alkermes' market research budget contracts as management bandwidth is consumed by integration.

Alkermes at a Glance

Dimension Detail
Company Alkermes, Inc. (NASDAQ: ALKS)
Headquarters Dublin, Ireland (US operations: Waltham, MA)
CEO Richard Pops (retiring Jul 31, 2026) → Blair Jackson (COO, effective Aug 1, 2026)
FY2025 Total Revenue ~$1.48B
FY2026 Revenue Guidance $1.73-1.84B
Proprietary Products Lybalvi, Aristada, Vivitrol, LUMRYZ (acquired Feb 2026)
Therapeutic Areas Psychiatry (schizophrenia, bipolar I), Addiction Medicine (OUD, AUD), Sleep Medicine (narcolepsy)
ZoomRx MSA Since July 21, 2015 (~11 years)
ZoomRx AO [Populate]
ZoomRx AD Sara Farina (Senior Manager)
Vinoth's role Overseen account
Active ZoomRx SOW value ~$456K (Lybalvi PET + Vivitrol PET) + Aristada SOV (ongoing)

Portfolio Summary

Lybalvi (olanzapine/samidorphan) — THE GROWTH ENGINE

  • Indications: Schizophrenia, Bipolar I Disorder
  • FY2025 revenue: $346.7M (+24% YoY)
  • FY2026 guidance: $380-400M
  • Key dynamic: 4-year real-world weight data (+1.47 kg) is the strongest long-term tolerability evidence in the atypical antipsychotic class. Cobenfy (BMS, muscarinic agonist) is the primary competitive threat but faces payer friction and ARISE adjunctive trial failure.
  • ZoomRx work: Multichannel PET ($338,701) — now tracking Cobenfy SoV alongside Vraylar, Caplyta, Rexulti

Aristada (aripiprazole lauroxil LAI) — MATURE/STABLE

  • Indication: Schizophrenia
  • FY2025 revenue: $370.0M (+7% YoY)
  • FY2026 guidance: $365-385M (flat)
  • Key dynamic: SOV stable at ~20% but Abilify Maintena/Asimtufii pulling away at ~40% SOV. Call plan contracted from 27.4K to 9.3K NPIs — Alkermes redirecting sales investment to Lybalvi.
  • ZoomRx work: SOV Tracker (ongoing quarterly, 125 HCPs/wave)

Vivitrol (naltrexone ER injection) — HARVEST/DEFEND

  • Indications: OUD (relapse prevention), AUD
  • FY2025 revenue: $467.9M (+2% YoY)
  • FY2026 guidance: $460-480M (flat)
  • Key dynamic: Generic entry (Teva) on January 15, 2027. Amneal authorized generic agreement signed Sep 2025. Sublocade ($856M) growing at 13% and pulling away.
  • ZoomRx work: PET ($117,435) — tracking vs. Sublocade and Brixadi

LUMRYZ (sodium oxybate ER) — NEW ACQUISITION

  • Indication: Narcolepsy (cataplexy, excessive daytime sleepiness)
  • Acquired: February 2026 via Avadel acquisition ($2.1B)
  • FY2026 guidance: $315-335M (~10.5 months)
  • Patients on therapy: ~3,100
  • Pipeline behind it: Alixorexton (orexin 2 receptor agonist, Breakthrough Therapy designation, Phase 3 starting Q1 2026)
  • ZoomRx work: None currently — potential new research opportunity

Corporate Context

Financial Profile (FY2025)

Metric FY2025
Total Revenue ~$1.48B
Proprietary Product Net Sales ~$1.2B (+9% YoY)
GAAP Net Income $241.7M
Adjusted EBITDA $394.0M
Cash & Investments $1.3B

FY2025 was a "transition year": Invega Sustenna US royalty expired, costing $215M. Proprietary product growth offset this, but total revenue was constrained. FY2026 adds LUMRYZ revenues ($315-335M) and represents the post-acquisition normalized baseline.

Strategic Pivot

  1. Avadel acquisition ($2.1B, Feb 2026): Adds LUMRYZ (narcolepsy) and alixorexton pipeline. Transforms Alkermes from psychiatry-only to broader CNS.
  2. CEO succession: Richard Pops retiring Jul 31, 2026 → Blair Jackson (COO) becomes CEO Aug 1. Pops stays as Board Chairman.
  3. Oncology divestiture: Sold oncology assets. Sold Athlone manufacturing facility. Tightening focus on psychiatry + sleep medicine.
  4. Vivitrol generic preparation: Amneal authorized generic agreement positions for managed decline rather than cliff.

SWOT Analysis

Strengths:

  • Lybalvi's unmatched 4-year metabolic safety data
  • Dual-indication Vivitrol franchise (only injectable for both OUD and AUD)
  • Aristada's dosing flexibility (monthly/6-week/2-month) and same-day initiation
  • $1.3B cash position enabling M&A
  • 11-year ZoomRx partnership provides deep competitive intelligence

Weaknesses:

  • Revenue concentration risk: top 3 brands are all CNS/psychiatry
  • Vivitrol generic entry in 9 months
  • Aristada losing SOV ground to Abilify Maintena/Asimtufii
  • LUMRYZ integration consuming management bandwidth
  • CEO transition during portfolio transformation

Opportunities:

  • LUMRYZ/sleep medicine as new ZoomRx research opportunity
  • Lybalvi PET Cobenfy tracking — unique competitive intelligence as first-mover in muscarinic class monitoring
  • Lybalvi bipolar I positioning (Cobenfy doesn't yet have BPD indication)
  • AUD market massively undertreated (2.5% medication rate) — Vivitrol growth vector
  • Alixorexton (Breakthrough Therapy) pipeline represents next-generation sleep opportunity

Threats:

  • Cobenfy label expansion to bipolar I (Phase 3 initiated 2025)
  • TEV-749 (Teva olanzapine LAI, NDA accepted Feb 2026) — threatens both Lybalvi and Aristada
  • Teva Vivitrol generic launch Jan 2027
  • Sublocade rapid induction label update (Feb 2025) eroding Vivitrol OUD positioning
  • CEO transition risk — new leadership may reassess vendor relationships and research priorities

What You Need to Know (Fast Orientation)

  1. Lybalvi is the only brand that matters for growth. Everything else is mature or declining. ZoomRx's value prop should increasingly center on Lybalvi competitive intelligence, especially Cobenfy monitoring.
  2. Vivitrol has a defined end date. Generic entry Jan 2027. Plan accordingly — the Vivitrol PET may not renew beyond FY2026.
  3. LUMRYZ is the BD opportunity. New acquisition, new TA (sleep medicine), no current ZoomRx work. The Avadel commercial team may need market research support during integration.
  4. CEO transition in August 2026. Blair Jackson (COO) takes over. Relationship continuity with research stakeholders needs validation.
  5. The account is narrow but stable. ~$456K in active SOWs with 11 years of MSA history. Not high-risk for churn, but not a high-growth account either unless LUMRYZ opens a new vector.
  6. Sara Farina leads the relationship day-to-day. CCA analytics team (Brian Gibbs PhD, Debasish Das, Melissa Dale) available for deeper work.

Accounts Hub | Home
